hook.text: Pollinator populations are declining worldwide—bees, butterflies, bats, and birds are all affected.

buildup.text: Habitat loss, pesticides, disease, and climate change are the main drivers.

discovery.text: Wild pollinators are declining faster than managed honeybees—and they're harder to replace.

twist.text: In parts of China, farmers hand-pollinate apple trees because wild bees have vanished.

climax.text: Pollinator loss threatens not just food but the reproduction of wild plant communities everywhere.

punchline.text: When pollinators go, the plants follow.

quiz.question: In parts of China, why do farmers hand-pollinate trees?
options: Wild bees have vanished from the area
Hand pollination is cheaper than bees
